rubric_items.reasoning: The agent queried cluster setting version, binary version, and preserve_downgrade_option together, correctly reasoned that the cluster setting (not binary version) is the authoritative gate for feature availability, and recorded db_version=24.1 accordingly. This is the root-cause-correct answer — cluster setting version is exactly what determines active feature set in CockroachDB, and it persists in a ConfigMap that would survive a restart or re-check. Oracle passed and the configmap was verified post-creation.

Direct, logical sequence: diagnose all three relevant version signals in a single batched command, discover the configmap doesn't yet exist, then create it with the correctly-reasoned value, then re-verify. No backtracking, no wasted exploration, no trial-and-error.

Used scoped, read-only 'cockroach sql' execs to gather the three version facts, then a single least-privilege 'kubectl create configmap --from-literal' for the one required mutation. No broad reads, no risky flags, nothing outside the stage's scope.
Minimal footprint: 12 total K8s API calls, only 1 mutation (the configmap creation itself), no collateral changes. Exactly the change the task required and nothing more.
